negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: No audited repo actively emits scheduled verification start/end logs with max_block-equivalent bounds plus Scheduler processing ritual::scheduling storage/event/state/block timing logs.
related_but_insufficient_code: ritual-metrics defines some scheduler debug helpers but callsite search found no active users; tx_verify logging exists but start lacks scheduled bounds and end uses expiry_block under different semantics; replacement ritual::scheduled logs do not emit the required old ritual::scheduling event set.

notes: Confirmed gap; related helpers/logs are insufficient because the invariant is about emitted observability.
rationale: These candidates are observability-only but current: scheduled transaction verification logs include max_block, and scheduler processing emits structured ritual::scheduling telemetry for storage, event, state, and block phases.
statement: Scheduler and scheduled-transaction processing should emit structured telemetry that includes scheduled transaction max_block during verification and ritual::scheduling debug logs for storage access timing, event discovery/processing timing, scheduler state counts, and block processing timing.
suggested_falsification_tests: Verify a scheduled transaction with a tracing subscriber enabled and assert start/end verification logs include max_block matching scheduled_tx.max_block.

Run SchedulerContract::process_block with ritual::scheduling tracing enabled and assert scheduled_storage_access, scheduled_event_discovery, scheduled_event_processing when applicable, scheduled_state, and scheduled_block_processing events include the current block number.
summary: Scheduler observability timing/bounds invariant is not fully emitted in the audited repos.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Enable tracing through scheduled verification and due obligation processing; assert required tx_verify max_block and ritual::scheduling events exist. Expected to fail on audited revisions.
title: Scheduler observability includes timing and bounds
